info.name: PatternForge API Playground
info.description:
Fully dynamic collection for all PatternForge REST endpoints.

**Setup**: Set `baseUrl` variable (default: http://localhost:8080). Run folders in order — IDs are auto-captured between requests via test scripts.

**Run order**: Use Collection Runner to execute all folders sequentially.

exec: // Capture first widget id for later requests
if (node.children) for (const c of node.children) { const r = findWidget(c); if (r) return r; }

request.description: Returns the full Composite tree rooted at 'root'. Auto-captures first widget ID into {{widgetId}}.
Creates a new container node under root. Stores the created container ID in {{containerId}}.

exec: pm.test('Response has id and type WIDGET', () => {

request.description: Creates a new widget node. Stores created widget ID in {{newWidgetId}}.
Nests {{newWidgetId}} inside {{containerId}}. Uses IDs captured from previous requests.
Renders the complete composite tree to HTML. Response includes htmlContent, renderTrace, metadata.
Deletes the widget created in 1.3. Uses {{newWidgetId}} captured automatically.

exec: // Fallback if widgetId not set by previous folder

request.description: Gets the current decorator stack for {{widgetId}}.
Removes all decorators from the widget at once (reset).
Uses LegacyGraphLib adapter. Stores chart widget id in {{chartWidgetId}}.
Uses OldChartLib adapter with CSV translation.
Uses NativeChartWidget directly — no adapter layer.
Shows the type conversion pipeline used by the adapter. Uses {{chartWidgetId}} from 3.1.
Orchestrates: LayoutEngine → ThemeEngine → RenderEngine in one call. Stores {{dashboardId}}.
NEW endpoint — was missing from previous collection.

Starts async 2-second load. Poll 5.1 again after ~2.5s to see LOADED state.
Renders using the renderer set in 6.2-6.4 (Bridge pattern in action).
